steps.title: Test a point
steps.content: A point is a solution if substituting its $x$- and $y$-coordinates makes $y<-3x+5$ true. The point's $y$-value must be less than the right side.

steps.title: Check the working point
steps.content:
For $(-3,0)$, substitute $x=-3$ and $y=0$:
$$
0<-3(-3)+5
$$
$$
0<14
$$
This is true, so the point is a solution.

steps.title: Eliminate the others
steps.content: The other choices fail because their $y$-values are not less than the matching right-side values. Only $(-3,0)$ satisfies the inequality. The answer is A.
